column17:
Kit Overview: Double the skills
✦ Cerydra selects one ally to receive Military Merit. The ally and Cerydra gain a flat SPD buff, and the ally also gets an ATK buff. When the ally deals damage, Cerydra will deal an instance of Additional DMG and gain 5 energy. This can activate up to 20 times, and the trigger count resets when using her Ultimate.

Cerydra gains a point of [Charge] when the ally with Military Merit takes action (+1), and when Cerydra uses her Skill (+1) or Ultimate (+2). 
Once reaching 6 points of [Charge], Military Merit upgrades to Peerage, the ally additionally gaining a Skill CRIT DMG and RES PEN buff, consuming 6 points of [Charge] and copies the skill performed by the ally with Military Merit. When used, Peerage will revert back to Military Merit.

Cerydra gives herself a 100% CRIT Rate and massive CRIT DMG buffs.

Synergies:
✦ DPS who primarily deal Skill DMG and can take action often.
Notes:
✦ The skill copied must deal damage to trigger.
✦ If charge is not at max, the ally with Military Merit can grant a stack through their ultimate once per battle.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Cerydra is a fairly strong support, however currently she struggles to find use outside of a very select few due to her buffs and how they work. Majority of other DPS would prefer many other options before even considering Cerydra.
column71:
Cerydra can only be used with DPS who primarily deal Skill DMG

We reccomend you only use her in the teams currently listed.

column9: Skill CRIT DMG, Res Pen, ATK, SPD

column59:

E1 provides an ally DEF Ignore, and restores their energy by 2 when Cerydra uses her skill

E2 increases both her and an ally's DMG dealt

Phainon specifically prefers her E1 because he'll be able to get 2 Coreflames per Cerydra skill, speeding up his ultimate recovery.

column17:
Kit Overview: Generalist Team-Wide Support with Rare buffs and DPS abilities.
Tribbie's Skill grant RES PEN, when other allies attack, regenerate energy for Tribbie, when they use Ultimate, Tribbie deal an AoE FUA, with a limit that can be reset after using Ultimate.

Tribbie gain energy whenever an ally hit enemies, the more enemies are hit, the more energy they gain.

Tribbie's Ultimate has the following effect: Launches an AoE attack, creates a [Zone] which inflict Vulnerability to all enemies, after an ally attacks, for every 1 target hit, deals 1 instance of Additional DMG to the target that has the highest HP among the hit targets.

Synergies:
✦  Allies that can frequently attack multiple targets to charge Tribbie's energy and deal more DMG.
✦  Allies that enjoy a support unit who can also attack multiple targets often.
✦  Teams that prefer having Res-Pen and Vulnerability for DMG amplification.
Notes:
✦  Since Tribbie's energy depend on how often and how many targets their allies attack, Tribbie's [Zone] can run into uptime issue in unfavorable situations.
Tips:
✦ Tribbie's potential for Ultimate spam makes them a good holder of Dance!Dance!Dance! Light cone if you can utilize it.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Tribbie are another universal Team-wide support unit that offer good and easy to manage buff, making them a good flex slot in most, if not all teams.

✦ Their main niche is for Blast/AoE teams, increasing the ST DMG output in multiple targets scenarios, when in a ST centric team or a low enemies count fight, Tribbie might not seem outstanding, 

✦ If you aren't interested in this niche and already have many flex support units, Tribbie can be on lower priority.
column71: Work in all teams so far as a flex slot.

column59: E1 > E2 =  S1 - Stop at E1 generally

column71: AoE + Ultimate Spam? Tribbie like that

column59:
E1: If the [Zone] is active, when allies attack enemies, they additionally deal 24% True DMG of the total DMG to the target with highest HP, a great increase to ST DMG output of the whole team.

E2: [Zone] deal 1 more instance of DMG per trigger and Increase the multiplier of Additional DMG by 120%.

S1 increase wearer's Crit DMG% by 36%, when wearer deals a FUA, regenerates 12 energy and buff team's Crit DMG by 48%, good for DPS Tribbie.

E1S0 Tribbie is great for vertical invest.

column17:
Kit Overview: Hypercarry support unit that get better with Summon Archetype.
Sunday lets 1 target and their summon (if they have one) to immediately take action, increases DMG% (more DMG% if the target has summon), Crit Rate% and dispels 1 debuff with his skill, lasting for 2 turns. His Ultimate applies [The Beatified] state, granting Crit DMG% and energy for the target.

His skill when used on ally with [The Beatified] state will no longer consume SP.

Synergies:
✦ As seen from how his abilities are introduced, it is clear that Sunday synergizes well with Summoner DPS-es, letting you skip the thought process of working with how fast or slow the summoned unit might be by simply advance it forward (e.g, Jing Yuan's Lightning Lord, Lingsha's Fuyuan).

✦ Outside of the Summon archetype, Sunday still works well in many situations, namely for Crit DMG teams and has potential to be being BiS teammate in many of them.

Notes:
✦ Sunday's kit only buffs a single ally target, making him exclusively a Hypercarry support. He is not the most suitable for multiple DPS teams.
✦ Sunday can not advance forward Harmony units with his skill, which limits certain setups.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Sunday is a very powerful and stable supports for DPS with summons or Remembrance DPS, Even without the summon aspect he is still one of the best supports for hypercarry teams, which makes him a very strong support to have in general
column59: Yes, but S1 is quite good

column71: Jing Yuan is so back - Jing Yuan Premium

column59: S1 > E1 > E2 - Stopping at E0S1 is fine

S1 ensures 3 turn Ultimate, gives more DMG% and makes Sunday become SP positive, unlocking more team options.
Sunday's S1 can also be used on other Harmony units.

E1: target of Skill gain 16% DEF ignore, their summon gains 40% DEF ignore for 2 turns, a strong buff for summoner.

E2: First usage of Ultimate regenerates 2 SP, [The Beatified] gets an additional 30% DMG%, the SP effect is mostly for challenge clears and you already gain a good amount of DMG% normally, so E2 and above is lower priority.

column71: Sunday with Break!? - Boothill the GOAT

column17:
Kit Overview: Team-wide buffer with Sub-DPS capacity
Robin buffs DMG% with her skill and Crit-DMG% for everyone from her talent. Additionally, when an attack from allies is unleashed, regenerates 2 energy for Robin.

When activating Ultimate, Robin enters [Concerto] state: lets all allies immediately take action, buffs their ATK by a great amount based on her own ATK, extra Crit DMG% buff for FUA, and deal Additional Damage with 100% Crit Rate when an ally attacks, until the [Concerto] state ends.

Synergies:
✦ Units that attack frequently to charge Robin's energy and deal damage during [Concerto].
✦ Any unit that can take advantage of her buffs, notably the huge ATK increase.
✦ Characters or Light Cones that grant energy in case her Ultimate's up-time is struggling.

Notes:
✦ When [Concerto] is active, Robin cannot take action, which means she will not take turns often to follow a specific rotation like most characters, lowering self-energy and SP production.
✦ With her Ultimate costing 160 Energy, not every team will be able to utilize Robin comfortably. Getting hit and energy buffs from allies, Light Cones and turbulences may compensate for this.

Tips:
✦ Energy-granting abilities like Tingyun's or Sunday's Ultimate can be used on Robin to activate her Ultimate early; letting allies taking more set of turns may give good results when the timing is tight.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ As long as you have DPS units that utilize her buffs, especially the ATK buff, getting Robin will be a good choice. The additional damage Robin has also helps out with damage check.

✦ Her team-wide full Action Advance is no joke, it can be a life-changing buff that cuts down your clear time greatly.

✦ It's recommended to use Robin in teams that can constantly attack; namely teams with FUA units to have the most comfort in Ultimate uptime and performance. Characters/LC that help her through Energy Regeneration are also appreciated.

✦ If you can't keep Robin's energy up, using her might be uncomfortable, and thus her performance will average out in a long fight.
column71: Prioritize high attack frequency setup for the most comfort.
Single-Target Follow Up Attack Dual DPS with Robin
AoE FUA Dual DPS with Robin

column59:
E1 provides team-wide 24% Res-Pen buff when [Concerto] is activated. This buff is not easily diluted and has great value for brute forcing type-resistance.

S1 provides ERR%, ATK% and DMG%, but it can be low priority if you have Bronya's LC or other Energy sources.

E2: ally's attacks now regenerate 3 energy for Robin and gain 16% Speed buff during [Concerto], bringing more speed stat and gameplay comfort.

E1S0 is enough for vertical investment.

column71: Huohuo's energy for Miss Robin - Sunday Robin Huohuo for any DPS
Main DPS Robin? (Requires high vertical investment)
She is a support, she fits anywhere (that can use her buffs and/or AA)

column17:
Kit Overview: Burst SP regeneration and High Crit DMG buffing support 
Sparkle's skill advances 1 ally's action forward by 50% and buff their Crit DMG% by a significant amount. 

Her talent increase the max number of Skill Points (SP) by 2, buff ATK% (extra ATK% for Quantum units), and provides DMG% buff when allies spend SP with a cap.
Finally, Sparkle's Ultimate regenerates 4 SP and grant all allies [Cipher], further increasing the Talent's DMG% Buff.

Synergies:
✦ Team Setups or DPSs that appreciate the Burst SP generation (ie. DHIL, Qingque)
✦ Any DPS that make use of the 50% Action Forward effectively along with her various buff, notably Crit DMG%
Notes:
✦ Sparkle's Crit DMG% buff only last until the start of the next turn. This means it can have bad uptime in teams with DPS that take turns often, including when they are paired with other Action Forward Supports.
✦ Sparkle's Action Forward effect is only 50%, which makes her best used along with slow DPS, and can be worse compared to someone like Bronya to cut Action Value (ie -1 setup)
Tips:
✦ Sparkle can be used in Dual DPS setup as she has some team-wide buff.
✦ With Energy Regeneration Rate% Rope equipped, Sparkle will always have a 3-turn Ultimate rotation, which can let you explore many other LC option, notably Dance!Dance!Dance!
column45:
✦ If you like a character, get them!

✦ Sparkle is a easy to use supports unit, just let her use her skill to pull up a slow DPS then press Ultimate when it's ready, even if you don't make use of the Action Value skip well, her strong buffs will let you achieve good result.

✦ Her buff up time can be an issue in certain team set up, but for general usage, Sparkle remains a great generalist choice.

✦ If you use a SP hungry team, she is an super comfortable choice.
column71: Fits anywhere that can use her buff well, usually Hypercarry w/ Crit DPS
I love not building Speed on my DPS

column59:
S1 gives Crit Rate% & Crit DMG% for the whole battle. It’s great for Dual DPS setups and DPS that deal DMG out of turns. Sunday's S1 lets her regen more SP, and excels with Hypercarries.

E1 extends [Cipher] duration by 1 turn and gives 40% ATK% team-wide buff, a nice buff as Sparkle's initial ATK% buff is low. E2 provides up to 24% DEF ignore, decent alone and gets better with more DEF shred sources.

Sparkle's early Eidolons don't particularly stand out, but are still respectable increases that make her a better team-wide buffing generalist.

column17:
Kit Overview: Superbreak Enabler and Break DMG buffer
When Harmony Main Character/Trailblazer uses their Skill, they deal significant Imaginary Toughness Damage in a Bounce formation for 7 times.

When HMC uses Ultimate, they grant all allies [Backup Dancer] for 3 of HMC's turns. Allies with [Backup Dancer] has their Break Effect increased, and can now deal Super Break damage. The Super Break damage multiplier increases the less enemies are on the field

If HMC is on field, all allies have their Break Effect increased, when enemies are Weakness Broken, they get action delayed more and generate Energy for HMC 

Synergies:
✦ Best teammate for Break or Super break teams, since they enable the ability to deal damage beyond the initial Break and increases Super Break damage

Notes:
✦ Their potential for Ultimate spamming makes HMC a very potent Dance! Dance! Dance! holder.
column45:
✦ HMC is great, if not best for Super Break and break teams in general, it is recommended to build them if you use Break/Super Break teams a lot.

✦ HMC E6 is completely free so try to get them if you want to play Break/Super Break archetype.

✦ HMC has a few notable upgrades in terms of LC:
 1) Dance! Dance! Dance!: HMC can have very high uptime on Ultimate if the team can break enemies often, which make them one of the best Dance! Dance! Dance! abuser. However this LC has high demands of player's skill to properly be used

 2) Ruan Mei S1: This LC gives a lot of BE%, energy per wave and SP regeneration, which makes this a very solid option for HMC. However it is not recommended to get Ruan Mei S1 specifically for HMC.

 3) In Pursuit of the Wind: This is a Battle pass LC, which directly increases break DMG. This is a very great damage buff for Break/Super Break teams if you do not need extra utilities.
column71: Works in about any break team

column17:
Kit Overview: Strong team damage amplifier.
When Ruan Mei is on field, she grants the whole team Break Effect and SPD. Whenever an enemy is weakness broken, Ruan Mei deals an additional damage based on her Break damage.

Her Skill grants Weakness Break Efficiency and Damage bonus to the whole team.With her Ultimate, she deploys a field. In this field, allies will gain All-type RES PEN and attacked enemies will be inflicted with Thanatoplum Rebloom. This debuff delays enemies action further when they try to recover from weakness broken state.

Synergies:
✦ Best teammate for Break teams.
✦ Works well with any teams that appreciate her teamwide buff.

Notes:
✦ The DMG% buff from her A6 Trace does not need to be maximised in Break teams, as it does not amplify Break damage.
✦ Ruan Mei is typically built to be fast, however she can opt for slower builds for low cycle/low AV clears.

Tips:
✦ Ruan Mei's technique is powerful in SU/DU for giving you a head start by reducing enemies toughness and dealing significant damage based on the amount of blessings that you have.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Ruan Mei is a core member of Break and Superbreak teams, making her a high priority pull for this archetype.

✦ Her long list of synergies and unique buffs makes her almost always a strong option, though other amplifiers such as Robin, Sparkle, and Jiaoqiu can still be higher priority depending on what teams are used most.

✦ Ruan Mei is one of the best Support for Apocalyptic Shadow.
column71: Works anywhere as a team damage amplifier.

column59:
E1 provides a strong 20% Def Ignore to her team when her Ult is up, which benefits all damage types.

E2 is not recommended to aim for

S1 provide SP when using Ultimate and give the team DMG% (not Break DMG%) but locks her out of consistent 3 turn Ult rotation, which makes you rely on getting hit, it's a strong option when utilized well, but S5 Memories of The Past is comparable.

column71: She works for any team, she's a Support

column17:
Kit Overview: Hypercarry support unit with potent Action Advances.
Bronya's Skill lets 1 target immediately take action, increases their DMG% and dispels 1 debuff with her Skill, the buff lasting for 1 turn. Her ultimate grants Crit DMG% and ATK for the entire team.
While on the field, Bronya also buffs her team's DMG%.

Her Basic Attack also advances her action forward, making it useful for certain rotations.

Synergies:
✦ Bronya is great for units who highly value action advance and deals majority of their damage on turn.

Notes:
✦ Bronya's kit only buffs her target for low amounts of turns, making her mostly a Hypercarry support. She is not the most suitable for multiple DPS teams.
✦ Bronya without E1 and/or S1/Sunday S1 can be very SP intensive, requires good SP management in order to maximize her potential.
✦ Due to her short buff duration, Bronya should be used with DPS who deals damage on-turn.

Tips:
✦ Bronya can also advance Harmony units, unlike Sunday, so using her to advance Sunday -> your main DPS is a very viable team comp.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Bronya can be obtained from Standard banner or from losing 50/50 so you do not need to specifically aim to get her.
column71:
Work for most Crit DPS and DPS that can use her full Action Advance,
be aware of her SP consumption even with her E1 and S1 however

Due to how SP hungry Bronya is, E1 and/or S1 are very high value if you use Bronya often, her subsequent eidolons are fine, which can unlock some extra synergy with certain DPS but usually not needed.
